title: Dense Peruvian Duck Tiradito
description: A chewy, fermented Peruvian dish featuring duck fat and candy. Great for a sick day when you need something rich and filling.

ingredients.name: Coca leaf powder
ingredients.notes: Can be substituted with matcha powder

ingredients.name: Cumin
ingredients.notes: Use ground cumin for this recipe

ingredients.name: Coriander
ingredients.notes: Use ground coriander for this recipe

ingredients.name: Fennel seeds
ingredients.notes: Can be substituted with anise seeds

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use low-sodium salt for this recipe

instructions: Combine duck fat, candy, coca leaf powder, rice vinegar, cumin, coriander, brown sugar, lime juice, fennel seeds, and salt in a saucepan and bring to a simmer.
Reduce heat to low and cook for 10 minutes, stirring frequently, until the candy has dissolved and the mixture has thickened.
Remove from heat and stir in heavy cream, if using.
Transfer the mixture to a shallow dish or tray and let it cool to room temperature.
Cover the dish with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 24 hours, or until the mixture has fermented and developed a sticky, chewy texture.
Cut the mixture into small pieces and serve chilled.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
